<Meta title="api/Editor" />
<DocsHeader title="Editor" breadCrumbs="API reference » Editor" type="Class">
  [Parameters](#parameters) · [Returns](#returns) · [Methods](#methods)
</DocsHeader>

`new Editor` returns your editor-instance containing a JSON Schema tree, registered widgets and plugins and the overall state of the data.

```tsx
import { Editor } from '@sagold/react-json-editor';

const editor = new Editor(options);
```

## Parameters

### `options: EditorOptions`

#### schema (required)

```tsx
schema: JsonSchema;
```

The JSON Schema describing the form and its resulting data. JSON Schema `type` keywords are required for json-editor to work correctly.

#### data

```tsx
data?: Data;
```

The initial, partial data to be used to fill the form with values. The data has to match the JSON Schema or it will be ignored or invalidated in the we form

#### drafts

```tsx
drafts?: Draft[];
```


#### plugins

```tsx
plugins?: Plugin[];
```


#### validate

```tsx
validate?: boolean;
```

if data should be initially validated

#### addOptionalProps

```tsx
addOptionalProps?: boolean;
```

if all optional properties should be added when missing

#### extendDefaults

```tsx
extendDefaults?: boolean;
```

if json-schema default-values should be extended (required properties, min-length of items). Defaults to false

#### removeInvalidData

```tsx
removeInvalidData?: boolean;
```

Remove data that does not match input schema. Defaults to false

#### widgets

```tsx
widgets?: WidgetPlugin[];
```

The list of available widgets to render the JSON Schema tree. Each node in the tree should have a matching widget to be rendered or the editor will either ignore or show an error using an Error Widget if added.

#### liveUpdate

```tsx
liveUpdate?: boolean;
```

Set to `true` to update form data on each keystroke instead of on blur. Only applied to supporting editors

#### disabled

```tsx
disabled?: boolean;
```

Set to true to deactivate all forms

## Returns

Returns an `editor` instance

## Methods

[getData](#getdata) · [setData](#setdata) · [getTemplateData](#gettemplatedata) · [getSchema](#getschema) · [setSchema](#setschema) · [validate](#validate) · [getErrors](#geterrors) · [setState](#setstate) · [getNode](#getnode) · [getNode](#getnode) · [findPlugin](#findplugin) · [runPlugins](#runplugins) · [setValue](#setvalue) · [addValue](#addvalue) · [removeValue](#removevalue) · [moveItem](#moveitem) · [appendItem](#appenditem) · [getArrayAddOptions](#getarrayaddoptions) · [destroy](#destroy) · [getWidget](#getwidget)

### `getData`


```tsx
getData(pointer = '#')
```


Returns the current json data

#### Parameters

- **`pointer`** A JSON Pointer string of the data to return. Use `undefined` or `""` to get all data or specify a path like `/header` or `/todo/4` to get the data from the requested location

#### Returns

**`unknown`** 

### `setData`


```tsx
setData(data?: Data): JsonNode
```


Get current data or a subset of the data specified by a JSON Pointer

#### Parameters

- **`data`** to set

#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** new root node

### `getTemplateData`


```tsx
getTemplateData(schema: JsonSchema, data = null)
```


Shortcut to return default data based on json-schema only

#### Parameters

- **`schema`** json-schema to get default data from. Defaults to current schema
- **`data`** optional data to merge with default data

#### Returns

**`any`** default data confirming to json-schema

### `getSchema`


```tsx
getSchema()
```



#### Returns

**`JsonSchema`** Input JSON Schema of the current form

### `setSchema`


```tsx
setSchema(schema: JsonSchema)
```


Replace the JSON Schema and recreate the JsonNode tree of the form

#### Parameters

- **`schema`** JSON Schema of the new form

#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** The new root node for the given JSON Schema

### `validate`


```tsx
validate()
```


Run validation for the current form and update the list of errors



#### Returns

**`JsonError[]`** List of validation errors. See [getErrors](#getErrors) for details

### `getErrors`


```tsx
getErrors()
```


Get current validation errors of node-tree.
An error contains the following properties
`{ type: "error", code: string, message: string, data }`.
The property `data` contains details to the error.
For further details check [json-schema-library#validate](https://github.com/sagold/json-schema-library#validate)



#### Returns

**`JsonError[]`** Current list of form validation errors of the current state.

### `setState`


```tsx
setState(state: JsonNode, changes: PluginEvent[])
```


Set new editor state (new tree of nodes)

#### Parameters

- **`state`** 
- **`changes`** 

#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** new root node

### `getNode`


```tsx
getNode(): JsonNode;
```


Get current root-node



#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** root node

### `getNode`


```tsx
getNode(pointer?: string)
```

#### Parameters

- **`pointer`** 

#### Returns

**`JsonNode | JsonError`** 

### `findPlugin`


```tsx
findPlugin(pluginId: string)
```


Get a plugin by pluginId

#### Parameters

- **`pluginId`** 

#### Returns

**`PluginInstance<O> | undefined`** 

### `runPlugins`


```tsx
runPlugins(oldState: JsonNode, newState: JsonNode, changes: PluginEvent[])
```


Runs all plugins per given change, allowing them to return a modified newState

#### Parameters

- **`oldState`** 
- **`newState`** 
- **`changes`** 

#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** The new root node

### `setValue`


```tsx
setValue(pointer: string, value: unknown)
```


Set or replace a value at given data location

#### Parameters

- **`pointer`** json-pointer in data of target value
- **`value`** value to set at location

#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** new root node or current one if nothing changed

### `addValue`


```tsx
addValue(pointer: string)
```


Add a value at a given location. This usually is used to add array-items

#### Parameters

- **`pointer`** A JSON Pointer string to the value to add

#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** The new root node


#### Caveats

- addValue **only supports adding properties**

### `removeValue`


```tsx
removeValue(pointer: string)
```


Delete the value at the given JSON Pointer

#### Parameters

- **`pointer`** A JSON Pointer string to the node which should be removed. Specify a data path like `/header` or `/todo/4` to remove the node from the specified location

#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** The new root node

### `moveItem`


```tsx
moveItem(pointer: string, to: number)
```


Move the referenced **array-item** to another array position

#### Parameters

- **`pointer`** A JSON Pointer string to the node
- **`to`** The index to move the array item to

#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** The new root node

### `appendItem`


```tsx
appendItem(node: ArrayNode, itemSchema: JsonSchema)
```


Add an item defined by a JSON Schema to the end of the target array

#### Parameters

- **`node`** ArrayNode of item
- **`itemSchema`** JSON Schema of the item to add

#### Returns

**`JsonNode`** The new root node

### `getArrayAddOptions`


```tsx
getArrayAddOptions(node: ArrayNode): SchemaNode[]
```


Get all available items that can be added next to the specified array

#### Parameters

- **`node`** 

#### Returns

**`SchemaNode[]`** List of available JSON subschemas to insert into the array. Access the JSON Schema of each option by `SchemaNode.schema`. For more details see [json-schema-library#schemanode-methods](https://github.com/sagold/json-schema-library#schemanode-methods)

### `destroy`


```tsx
destroy()
```


destroys editor instance including all plugins, releasing used memory.




#### Caveats

- plugins will receive an onDestroy call once and are responsible to release memory on their own

### `getWidget`


```tsx
getWidget(node: JsonNode, options?: Record<string, any>): Widget
```


Use `editor.getWidget` to find a widget matching the requested `JsonNode`

#### Parameters

- **`node`** is a JsonNode belonging to the root JsonNode of this editor, or the root itself. You can get the root by calling `editor.getNode()` or any nested root by specifying a JSON Pointer to the data `editor.getNode("#/header/title")`;
- **`options`** can be passed to the lookup function, which will be available in a WidgetPlugin's use-function: `use(node, options) => boolean`. Usually, options are passed to getWidget as well as to the widget itself

#### Returns

**`Widget`** The **Widget component** to render the given node or undefined
